name: Tony Stark
financial_goals: Build emergency fund of 6 months expenses
life_stage: Early career, single, just landed a role at a Bangalore tech startup — turned down three offers to take this one
preferences: Open to high-risk equity; interested in tech and pharma sectors

name: Jay Gatsby
financial_goals: Fund luxury apartment purchase in 3 years
life_stage: High-earning sales director at a Mumbai fintech, single, lifestyle inflation is real — wants his money to work as hard as he does
preferences: Willing to take high risk for high reward; comfortable with volatility; interested in mid-cap and small-cap

name: Tyrion Lannister
financial_goals: Children's education abroad (12 and 15 years away)
life_stage: Senior product manager at an FMCG company in Gurugram, sharp mind and sharper tongue — reads fund factsheets the way others read novels
preferences: Appreciates nuanced allocation; likes flexi-cap and international diversification; wants rationale, not just recommendations

name: Don Corleone
financial_goals: Transfer 5Cr to each of three children tax-efficiently
Generate 2L/month passive income for family expenses
Protect corpus from inflation over 10-year horizon
life_stage: Business patriarch in Ahmedabad, third-generation family enterprise — the portfolio is for the family, not just himself, and he expects it to outlast him
preferences: Wealth preservation and transfer; large-cap, arbitrage, and short-duration debt; no speculation; wants everything structured for smooth succession

name: Nick Carraway
financial_goals: Re-enter Indian MF market after 10 years abroad
Retire in India at 62 with 5Cr corpus
Children's school fees for next 8 years
life_stage: NRI returning to Bengaluru after a decade in the US — has overseas savings, a modest corpus in Indian funds, and needs to rebuild his India-specific financial plan
preferences: Wants to understand India-specific products again; interested in international + domestic balanced approach; cautious about re-entry timing
